core_thesis: Bhattacharyya argues that the subject is known precisely as freedom from object: bodily, psychical and spiritual subjectivity are graded releases from objectification culminating in the demand for an intuition of the self beyond introspective function. Hence Advaita does not reduce the world to sheer nullity; the world is unreal apart from Brahman yet real in Brahman's reality, and the Absolute admits alternative formulations without ceasing to be absolute ([The Subject as Freedom, ch. 1](cite://kc-bhattacharyya/the-subject-as-freedom/1); [Studies in Philosophy II, Concept of the Absolute and Its Alternative Forms](cite://kc-bhattacharyya/studies-in-philosophy/2.absolute-alternative-forms)).
